title: Aircraft Interiors Manufacturing
entry_mechanism: PSAP Track 1 — International aircraft interiors manufacturer attracted to Saudi operations for Saudi carrier market
executive_summary:
Aircraft interiors — galleys, overhead bins, passenger seating, cabin sidewall panels, lavatories, and in-flight entertainment system enclosures — are manufactured by a global supply chain that is less technically restricted (no ITAR, no Nadcap requirement for most components) and more commercially accessible than structural aerostructures. Saudi Arabia's expanding commercial aircraft fleet — over 400 aircraft on order between Saudia, Riyadh Air, and Flynas — creates a captive domestic demand for cabin components that currently flows entirely to international manufacturers.

The aircraft interiors market was valued at $16.8 billion in 2024. Saudi Arabia's domestic refurbishment and line-fit cabin components opportunity is estimated at $150–250 million over the Phase I period. More significantly, Saudi Arabia's position as a growing aviation hub creates export potential: Egyptian, Jordanian, and African carriers with limited access to European and US interior suppliers represent an accessible regional market for a Saudi interiors manufacturer.

The manufacturing processes involved — fiberglass and composite panel fabrication, aluminium machining, foam and textile assembly, electronics integration — are all accessible to Saudi Arabia's industrial base with standards upgrades. FAA/EASA supplemental type certificates (STCs) for cabin modifications and DACH certification for seat structures are the primary regulatory milestones.

companies.name: Safran Cabin (formerly Zodiac)
companies.rationale: Safran Cabin is a global leader in aircraft galleys, lavatories, and cabin components. Safran has Saudi Arabia presence and the scale to anchor a Saudi interiors manufacturing JV.
companies.contact: Safran Cabin VP International Industrial Partnerships

companies.name: Collins Aerospace Interiors
companies.rationale: Collins supplies aircraft seats and IFE systems and has Saudi Arabia airline relationships. Some ITAR considerations but commercial cabin products are generally accessible.
companies.contact: Collins Aerospace VP Cabin Systems, International

companies.name: Recaro Aircraft Seating
companies.rationale: Recaro is a leading independent aircraft seat manufacturer. Non-ITAR German technology; DACH certified; active Middle East airline customer relationships.
companies.contact: Recaro Aircraft Seating VP International Business

companies.name: JAMCO Corporation
companies.rationale: JAMCO manufactures galleys and lavatories for Boeing and has established manufacturing partnerships internationally. Non-ITAR Japanese technology.

companies.name: Diehl Aviation
companies.rationale: Diehl Aviation produces overhead bins, cabin panels, and cabin management systems. Non-ITAR; active Gulf airline relationships.

partners.name: Saudia Technic / Engineering Division
partners.current: Cabin refurbishment and modification; some composite panel repair; EASA Part 145 for cabin work
partners.upgrade: Establish Safran Cabin JV for galley and lavatory manufacturing; STC for Saudi-designed cabin modifications; EASA DOA (Design Organisation Approval) for cabin design authority
partners.timeline: 24 months to first Saudi-manufactured cabin component

partners.name: KAEC Industrial Authority
partners.upgrade: Dedicated aircraft interiors manufacturing cluster at KAEC; shared composite fabrication and finishing infrastructure; GACA/EASA regulatory facilitation

partners.name: Saudi Textile and Industrial Companies
partners.upgrade: Aircraft-grade flame-retardant textile qualification (FAR 25.853); seat cover and upholstery manufacturing for Saudi carrier cabin refurbishment market

partners.name: TVTC Aviation Academy
partners.current: Aviation technician training; some cabin maintenance training
partners.upgrade: Aircraft interiors manufacturing technician programme; fiberglass and composite fabrication training; DACH seat assembly certification
